turns.user_message: My friend made a joke about how I'm always the last to know things. I used to just laugh it off, but today it stung a little.
I keep noticing how often I'm left out of plans or conversations now. It feels like I'm on the outside looking in most of the time.
I'm starting to believe that joke was true, and everyone else knows it too. The thought of being permanently out of the loop makes my chest feel tight.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This used to not bother the speaker.
